claim: For all integers n > 2, Euler's totient phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is prime, then phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or not is_prime(n) or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a power of 2, then phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0) or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a prime power, then phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (is_prime(vp(n, 2)) and vp(n, 2) > 0) or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, phi(n) is divisible by 2 but not by 4.
predicate: n <= 2 or phi(n) % 2 == 0 and phi(n) % 4 != 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a power of 2, then phi(n) is a power of 2.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0) or (phi(n) & (phi(n) - 1)) == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a prime power of 2, then phi(n) is a power of 2.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (is_prime(vp(n, 2)) and vp(n, 2) > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0) or (phi(n) & (phi(n) - 1)) == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is composite, then phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (not is_prime(n)) or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a product of two distinct primes, then phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (tau(n) == 4 and len([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) == 2) or phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a product of two distinct primes and one of them is 2, then phi(n) is a multiple of 4.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (tau(n) == 4 and len([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) == 2 and 2 in [p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) or phi(n) % 4 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a power of an odd prime, then phi(n) is even.
predicate: n <= 2 or not (is_prime(vp(n, 2)) and vp(n, 2) > 0 and vp(n, 2) % 2 != 0) or phi(n) % 2 == 0

predicate: n <= 2 or not (is_prime(vp(n, 2)) and vp(n, 2) > 0 and vp(n, 2) % 2 != 0) or phi(n) % (vp(n, 2) - 1) == 0
